rating_scale.COVERED: Topic has dedicated section(s) with detailed rules, checklists, and thresholds
rating_scale.PARTIALLY_COVERED: Topic is referenced or addressed indirectly but lacks dedicated comprehensive treatment
rating_scale.NOT_COVERED: Topic is absent or mentioned only in passing without actionable rules

stability.details: Comprehensive coverage of ICH Q1A-E plus 2025 draft. Storage conditions for all 5 climatic zones, extrapolation decision trees (5 scenarios), significant change criteria, photostability (Q1B), bracketing/matrixing (Q1D), statistical analysis (Q1E), WHO Zone IVb, ATMP-specific, biologicals, advanced kinetic modeling.

impurities.details: All 5 impurity frameworks covered: Q3A (organic DS), Q3B (degradation DP), Q3C (residual solvents with Class 1/2/3), Q3D (elemental impurities with PDE tables), Q3E (extractables/leachables with TTC/QT thresholds). Dose-dependent reporting/identification/qualification thresholds codified. M7 mutagenic impurities covered in cross-domain skill.

specifications.details: Q6A chemical substance specifications (universal tests, 8 decision trees), Q6B biotech product specifications (potency, purity, process-related impurities, product-related impurities). Both release and shelf-life specifications covered.

analytical_validation.details: Full Q2(R2)/Q14 analytical procedure lifecycle: ATP development, validation characteristics matrix (specificity, repeatability, intermediate precision, accuracy, range, DL, QL), minimum requirements, reportable ranges per method type, verification/trend monitoring, revalidation triggers.

process_validation.details: Q8-Q13 process validation framework including design space, PAR, RTRT. EU GMP Annex 15 qualification/validation. Standard vs non-standard process distinction. Minimum 3 production-scale batches. Continuous manufacturing validation per Q13. Cleaning validation with HBEL-based limits.

container_closure.details: Q3E extractables/leachables with AET calculation, safety concern thresholds (TTC, QT, local toxicity). Semi-permeable container stability testing. Ph.Eur. 3.1/3.2 series. EU Annex 1 100% CCIT for fusion-sealed <=100mL. Compatibility, sorption, leaching.

drug_substance_characterization.details: Q11 drug substance development including starting material selection (6 principles), impurity formation/fate/purge, physicochemical/biological properties. Q6A/Q6B specification-setting. Polymorphism, chirality, particle size decision trees.

drug_product_formulation.details: Q8(R2) pharmaceutical development: QTPP, CQA identification, excipient selection/compatibility, formulation strategy, overages justification. Enhanced (QbD) vs traditional approaches. Dissolution development. Novel excipient requirements (EU).

manufacturing_process.details: Q7 GMP for APIs (quality system, documentation, production, laboratory, validation, facilities). Q13 continuous manufacturing (RTD, material traceability, diversion, state-of-control, startup/shutdown). EU GMP Annex 1 sterile manufacturing (CCS, PUPSIT, cleanroom grades).

control_strategy.details: Dedicated Section 4 in quality systems skill: input material controls, process design, in-process controls, RTRT, CQA-linked justification, variability management, batch release requirements. Design space vs PAR distinction.

comparability.details: Q5E comparability exercise: 23 rules (CP-001 to CP-023). Covers exercise design, 5 quality dimension comparison, specification impact, stability bridging, process revalidation, viral safety retesting, drift monitoring, decision tree for nonclinical/clinical bridging. Biosimilar comparability also covered in EU GMP skill.

trial_design.details: 583 rules from E1, E3, E4, E7, E8(R1), E9/E9(R1), E10, E20. Study objectives, hypotheses, type classification, target population, control group selection (7 types with criteria), randomization, blinding, CSR structure.

endpoints.details: Phase 3 of clinical design skill: primary endpoints (clinical meaning, measurement properties, timing, composite criteria), secondary endpoints (hierarchy, PROs with MCID), safety endpoints (exposure-adjusted rates, dose/duration-response).

statistical_methods.details: E9/E9(R1) comprehensive: estimands framework (5 attributes, 5 intercurrent event strategies), sample size justification, multiplicity control (hierarchical, gatekeeping, graphical), interim analyses (alpha spending, DMC), power thresholds, significance levels.

safety_reporting.details: 823 lines covering E2A-E2F, E14, E19-E23. ICSR minimum validity (4 elements), E2B(R3) XML data elements, null flavor usage, PBRER (20 sections), DSUR (20 sections with 12-field line listings), expedited reporting timelines, MedDRA coding requirements.

pharmacovigilance.details: ICH E2E pharmacovigilance planning (safety specification 4 modules, PV plan, action plans). EU GVP Modules I, II, V, VII, VIII, IX, XV, XVI: QPPV, PSMF, RMP structure, PSUR/EU appendix, PASS protocols, signal management, DHPC, risk minimisation measures with effectiveness evaluation.

dose_response.details: E4 dose-response: minimum 3 dose levels, 5 design types (parallel, crossover, forced/optional/placebo-controlled titration), exposure-response analysis, food effects, drug interaction effects, organ impairment effects, elderly-specific dose-response.

special_populations_pediatric.details: E11(R1) and E11A: extrapolation concept/plan, 5 age subsets (preterm neonates through adolescents), 3-tier similarity assessment, M&S/Bayesian methods, age-appropriate formulation, sparse sampling, pediatric-specific safety endpoints, long-term follow-up, regulatory plans (PIP/PSP).

special_populations_elderly.details: E7: minimum 100 patients aged >=65, no arbitrary upper age limit, PK assessment for age-related changes (renal, hepatic, protein binding), drug interactions in polypharmacy context, efficacy/safety subgroup analysis, dose recommendations. Fragile elderly (>75) addressed in E12 antihypertensive section.

special_populations_pregnant.details: E21 pregnancy/breastfeeding safety: WoE framework for inclusion, DART study evaluation, close monitoring, infant follow-up, breastmilk transfer, trimester-specific consent. EU CTR Art. 33 specific protections for pregnant/breastfeeding in clinical trials. Reproductive toxicity nonclinical coverage in S5(R3).

ethnic_factors.details: E5(R1): intrinsic/extrinsic factor classification, drug sensitivity Class A/B/C, CCDP evaluation, bridging study design, CYP polymorphism assessment. Japan-specific bridging requirements extensively covered in v3 synthesis and multi-jurisdiction skill.

adaptive_designs.details: E20: 39 rules. Adaptation classification (6 types: sample size re-estimation, treatment arm selection, population enrichment, endpoint modification, randomization ratio, seamless Phase 2/3). Type I error control, bias-adjusted estimation, operational integrity, simulation requirements, regulatory interaction.

biomarkers.details: E15 pharmacogenomic biomarker definitions (PGx/PGt distinction, coding categories). E16 biomarker qualification (context of use, 5-section CTD-aligned format, analytical performance, simultaneous multi-agency submission). E18 genomic sampling and data management.

toxicology_acute.details: Acute toxicity is not addressed as a standalone study category. Per modern ICH M3(R2), single-dose toxicity studies are de-emphasized in favor of dose-escalation and repeat-dose studies. The nonclinical review framework covers general toxicology but does not include explicit acute toxicity study design criteria or checklists. Limit doses for acute studies (2000 mg/kg) are referenced only in genotoxicity context.

toxicology_chronic.details: Step 2: 6-month rodent + 9-month non-rodent for standard chronic. Biologics max 6 months (S6(R1)). Cancer drugs max 3 months (S9). Species selection, study design quality, recovery groups, route matching, high dose selection, TK integration.

toxicology_reproductive.details: Step 6: S5(R3) FEED (fertility, 16/sex, 4 groups), EFD (embryo-fetal, 2 species, full fetal evaluation), PPND (pre/postnatal, F1 behavioral/reproductive assessment). Exposure margin interpretation (<10-fold = concern, 10-25 = moderate, >25 = minor). Cancer drug and biologic exemptions.

toxicology_carcinogenicity.details: Step 5: S1A need assessment (>=6 month continuous use trigger), S1B(R1) WoE assessment (6 factors), rasH2-Tg 6-month mouse alternative, S1C(R2) dose selection (6 high-dose criteria, 90-day dose-ranging). Cancer drug and biologic exemptions codified.

toxicology_genetic.details: Step 4: S2(R1) standard battery (Option 1: Ames + in vitro mammalian + in vivo MN; Option 2: Ames + two-tissue in vivo). All 5 Ames strains, top concentration limits, positive result follow-up (WoE, two-in-vivo-tissue). Biologic exemptions. Cancer drug modifications.

safety_pharmacology.details: Dedicated 332-line skill: S7A core battery (CNS, CV, respiratory), S7B cardiac safety (hERG, in vivo QT, follow-up studies), E14/S7B integrated proarrhythmic risk assessment (5 criteria for low TdP risk), supplemental studies, modality-specific packages, timing alignment with clinical development. Decision trees for TQT waiver and follow-up study triggers.

pk_adme.details: Toxicokinetics (S3A) covered within general toxicology: AUC, Cmax, sampling timepoints, exposure margins, microsampling. Metabolite assessment addressed in safety pharmacology (disproportionate metabolites, >10% threshold). However, standalone nonclinical PK/ADME studies (absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion as per S3B) lack a dedicated evaluation framework. Biodistribution for gene therapy (S12) is well-covered. Clinical PK is addressed through E4/E7 but nonclinical ADME characterization studies are not systematically treated.

ectd_structure.details: Module 1-5 structural completeness checks. eCTD v4.0 format per 21 CFR 314.50/601.14. M8 eCTD lifecycle management rules. Cross-module consistency verification. EU-specific Module 1 variations. Japan CTD-JP Module 1.

nda_requirements.details: Complete 21 CFR mapping (Parts 1, 11, 50, 54, 56, 58, 200, 201, 207, 210/211, 312, 314, 320, 600). NDA-specific: adequate/well-controlled study criteria (314.126), safety update (314.50(d)(5)(vi)(b)), post-approval supplement categories (314.70). CRL deficiency taxonomy from 419 CRLs.

bla_requirements.details: BLA quality section analysis (Q5A-E, 147 rules). BLA vs NDA CRL frequency comparison (60.2% vs 35% facility deficiency). 21 CFR Part 600 series mapped. Biologic product deviation reporting. Manufacturing discontinuance notice. BLA-specific Module 3 requirements.

maa_requirements.details: 412 structured legal requirements from 8 EU legislative instruments. Application dossier per Art. 8(3). Centralised/MRP/DCP pathway determination. SmPC format. Environmental risk assessment. PIP requirement. RMP mandatory for all new MAs. QP batch release. Variations classification (Reg 1234/2008).

jnda_requirements.details: Foreign manufacturer accreditation (PAL Art. 13-3, 5-year renewal, 5-month timeline). Bridging study requirement (E5). Japanese patient enrollment. Re-examination system (8yr NCE, 10yr orphan). CTD-JP Module 1. Japanese MAH requirement. DDI transporter panel. CYP2C19 PM emphasis.

labeling.details: FDA PI 17-section format (21 CFR 201.56/201.57). EU SmPC 12-section QRD template with Braille. Canada PM 3-part bilingual format with ~70 XML validation rules. Japan Tenpu Bunsho format. WHO label prohibitions. CRL analysis showing labeling as #1 deficiency at 92.7%. Four incompatible format systems mapped.

post_approval_changes.details: Q12 lifecycle management: Established Conditions, reporting categories (PA/CBE-30/Annual Report mapped for FDA), PACMP, PLCM. EU variations classification (Type IA/IB/II/extension, Reg 1234/2008). Q14 analytical procedure changes. Urgent safety restrictions. Japan manufacturer change notification.

gmp.details: Q7 API GMP (quality system, documentation, production, laboratory, validation, facilities, biotech-specific). Q10 pharmaceutical quality system. EU GMP Annex 1 (2022 sterile, 16-element CCS), Annex 2 (biologics), Annex 15 (qualification/validation). 21 CFR 210/211. EU-specific HBEL cleaning validation, QP certification, PQR requirements.

fda_vs_ema_divergences.details: 68 mapped EU-FDA divergences across 15 categories in eu-vs-fda-divergences.json. EU regulatory skill has 15+ critical divergence flags (DTC advertising, QP batch release, RMP mandatory, data exclusivity 8+2+1, PIP timing, trial archival 25yr, trial insurance, non-serious ADR reporting, PSUR system, Braille, safety features, supply continuity, environmental risk, package leaflet testing). EU GMP skill maps 20 FDA-to-EU gaps. EU PV skill compares 10 PV system differences.

fda_vs_pmda_divergences.details: 17 mapped Japan-specific divergences with severity ratings. Key areas: bridging study requirement (Japan only), foreign manufacturer accreditation (5-month, Japan only), re-examination system, broader DDI transporter panel (OCT1, MRP2, MRP4, BSEP), CYP2C19 PM emphasis, Japanese MAH requirement, Kampo/crude drug monographs, patent non-linkage (Japan) vs linkage (US).

ema_vs_pmda_divergences.details: Cross-cutting divergence table in v3 synthesis covers EMA vs PMDA differences: QP batch certification (EU only), QPPV with personal liability (EU only), Annex 1 sterile manufacturing (EU only), bridging requirement (Japan only), manufacturer accreditation (Japan only), re-examination system (Japan only), patent non-linkage (both EU and Japan vs US/Canada). PV system architecture comparison shows EU stands alone with PSMF/PRAC/EudraVigilance.

canada_differences.details: Comprehensive 200-line skill: NDS/ANDS/SNDS submission types, PM Version 7 (3-part bilingual XML format, ~70 validation rules), CRP requirement (must be Canadian-marketed), CS-BE (Canada-only document), Establishment Licence, CTA requirements (PSEAT-CTA, bilingual consent, provincial QI licensing), 17 Canada-specific rejection reasons, FDA-to-Canada and EMA-to-Canada conversion checklists, patent linkage via PM(NOC), SGBA Plus, brand name confusion assessment.

who_requirements.details: WHO prequalification programme scope, Zone IVb stability (30C/75%RH), 2-batch allowance for generics, semi-permeable container low-RH protocol, label prohibitions ('ambient conditions'/'room temperature'), ongoing stability requirements, climatic zone coverage (all zones I-IVb), ICH Q1F withdrawal context, Zone II-to-IV extrapolation. WHO TRS 1010 Annex 10 and TRS 986 Annex 2 referenced.
